title: STUDIOLEE Badge Assembly
style: 1930s Fleischer Brothers rubber hose animation — hand-drawn ink outlines on vintage parchment, noodly boneless limbs, extreme squash and stretch, pie-cut eyes, oversized white gloves, warm sepia/ivory palette with lime green pops, watercolor grain, 24fps hand-drawn feel
method: First frame + last frame, Seedance fills the motion. Characters described in prompt, not rigidly referenced — model has creative freedom.
audio_rule: SFX only — cartoon whooshes, rubbery boings, spring sounds, mechanical whirring, one satisfying metallic click as badge completes. No music, no dialogue.

creative_brief.concept: The STUDIOLEE badge logo assembles itself — cartoon characters fly in from all directions with rubber hose physics and settle into their positions around the airplane
creative_brief.punchline: The badge pulses with warm golden light as everything locks in — alive and spinning
creative_brief.tone: Playful, chaotic, energetic — pure 1930s cartoon joy
creative_brief.setting: White/parchment background, circular badge as the stage
creative_brief.key_detail: Each character represents a STUDIOLEE service (SEO, chatbot, automation, etc.) — they are objects-with-personality, not humans
creative_brief.escalation: Empty badge → chaos as characters arrive → satisfying lock into final composition
creative_brief.notes: YouTube intro/outro. Must feel alive even when 'complete' — gentle spinning, wobbling, breathing.

main.name: Airplane Pilot
main.role: center of badge — stays in place while characters arrive
main.notes: 1930s Cuphead-style cartoon airplane with smiling pilot, lime green body, SL branding

supporting.name: Windmill
supporting.notes: Dutch windmill with spinning blades, walking legs, brown boots

supporting.name: Gear
supporting.notes: Smiling gear/cog character, waving, smaller gears around it

supporting.name: Disco Ball
supporting.notes: Dancing disco ball with sunglasses and gold chain

supporting.name: Washing Machine
supporting.notes: Walking washing machine with clothes flying out the top

supporting.name: Globe
supporting.notes: Globe with magnifying glass, searching expression

supporting.name: Record Player
supporting.notes: Victrola/record player grooving with music notes

supporting.name: Ferris Wheel
supporting.notes: Dizzy ferris wheel character with spinning wheel

assets._instructions: Starting frame and ending frame are the key assets. Character reference sheet is a loose style guide only — model has creative freedom on how characters look and move.

starting-frame.description: Empty circular badge — STUDIOLEE text arc top, LAAT HET DRAAIEN text arc bottom, airplane center, no characters

ending-frame.description: Complete badge2 logo with all characters in position around the airplane

character-reference-sheet.description: 3x3 grid of all 9 characters — used as loose style reference, NOT rigid template

scenes.name: Badge Assembly
scenes.description: Empty badge comes alive — characters burst in from all directions with rubber hose physics, chaos, then settle into final badge composition

first_frame.prompt: Empty circular vintage badge with STUDIOLEE text arched top, LAAT HET DRAAIEN bottom, 1930s cartoon airplane center, warm parchment colors

video.seedance_prompt:
STYLE: 1930s Fleischer Brothers rubber hose animation. Hand-drawn ink outlines on vintage parchment paper. Noodly boneless limbs, no elbows or knees. Extreme squash and stretch. Oversized white gloves, pie-cut eyes, big toothy grins. Warm sepia and ivory with pops of lime green. Watercolor grain. Everything alive.

A circular vintage badge logo with a cartoon airplane in the center starts empty and still. Then it comes to life:

The propeller spins faster and faster. Suddenly, wild cartoon characters — a bouncing windmill, a spinning gear, a dancing disco ball with sunglasses, a waddling washing machine with clothes flying out, a cheerful helicopter buzzing overhead, a wobbling globe with a magnifying glass, a marching hourglass, a grooving record player, and a dizzy ferris wheel — all come tumbling, bouncing, rolling, and flying into frame from every direction. Full rubber hose chaos. They crash into each other, bounce off the badge circle, overshoot and snap back like rubber bands. Squash flat on impact, stretch tall on jumps. Pure cartoon energy.

They gradually spiral into position around the circular badge, each finding their spot. As the last character locks in, the text STUDIOLEE arcs across the top and LAAT HET DRAAIEN arcs across the bottom. A warm golden pulse ripples through the badge. Everything gently spins in place. Complete and alive.

SFX only — cartoon whooshes, rubbery boings, spring sounds, mechanical whirring, one satisfying metallic click as the badge completes.

generations.result: Characters too static and literal — too many reference images made the model rigid
Fewer refs, characters described in prompt, more creative freedom

rules.1: NEVER batch-generate. One frame at a time, sequential, wait for approval.
rules.2: NEVER skip the image step. Image first, then video. Two separate steps.
rules.3: Characters described in prompt — only pass 2-3 reference images max to avoid rigidity.
rules.4: Image prompts = frozen moments. No motion verbs.
rules.5: Upload to GitHub IMMEDIATELY after generation.
rules.6: Let the model be creative — references are style guides, not rigid blueprints.
rules.7: Document every prompt, every generation, and every approval in this file.
rules.8: This is a branding asset — quality over speed.
